per_question_scores.question: What is the process of chunking in the context of RAG systems?
per_question_scores.answer: Chunking in the context of RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) systems is the process of dividing source documents into smaller units before embedding and indexing them. This division helps in managing the data more efficiently and can significantly impact the performance of the RAG system in terms of retrieval precision, recall, context preservation, storage cost, latency, and answer quality.
contexts:
Deep Dive: RAG Chunking Strategies
Chunking is the process of dividing source documents into smaller units before embedding and
indexing them. The chunking strategy directly affects retrieval precision, recall, context
preservation, storage cost, latency, and answer quality. This document explains how each major
chunking strategy works internally, its implementation logic, advantages, disadvantage
